subtopics: Chordates: Pisces, Amphibia, Reptilia, Aves, Mammalia

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct criterion for classifying animals into Protostomia and Deuterostomia?
The water canal system is a characteristic feature of which phylum?
Which animal shows true metameric segmentation?
Flame cells are excretory structures found in:
Which of the following is an acoelomate animal?
Radial symmetry is found in which of the following?
Which phylum is characterized by the presence of nematocysts?
The body cavity of Ascaris is:
Open circulatory system is found in:
Which of the following animals is viviparous?
Study the diagram of cross-section of an animal body showing different types of body cavities. Identify the pseudocoelomate condition.
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section diagrams of animal bodies showing: (A) solid mesoderm with no cavity (acoelomate), (B) body cavity between mesoderm and endoderm (pseudocoelom), (C) body cavity lined by mesoderm on both sides (true coelom), (D) haemocoel filled with blood

questions.question: Notochord is present throughout life in:
Which class of Chordata is characterized by three-chambered heart with one incompletely divided ventricle?
Aristotle's lantern is found in:
Which of the following is correctly matched?
The diagram shows the life cycle of Obelia. Identify the free-swimming larval stage.
questions.diagram_description: Life cycle diagram of Obelia showing polyp colony, medusa bud formation, free-swimming medusa, gamete release, fertilization, and planula larva stages with arrows indicating sequence

questions.question: Malpighian tubules are the excretory organs of:
Which of the following animals belongs to class Chondrichthyes?
Pneumatic bones are found in:
Which is the only egg-laying mammal from the following?
Refer to the diagram showing different types of symmetry in animals. Which symmetry is shown by adult echinoderms?
questions.diagram_description: Diagrams showing: spherical symmetry (any cut gives equal halves), radial symmetry (cuts along central axis give equal halves), bilateral symmetry (only one plane gives equal halves), and asymmetry (no plane gives equal halves)

questions.question: Canal system in sponges is helpful in:
Which of the following has a two-chambered heart?
The study of the diagram of Ascaris shows pseudocoelom. This body cavity develops from:
questions.diagram_description: Transverse section of Ascaris showing outer cuticle, hypodermis, muscle layer, pseudocoelom (fluid-filled space), and internal organs including intestine and reproductive organs, with labels indicating the spaces between tissue layers

questions.question: Animals belonging to phylum Arthropoda are characterized by:
Which phylum does Balanoglossus belong to?
Haemocoel is characteristic of:
The diagram shows the cross-section of a starfish arm. Identify the water vascular system component.
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of starfish arm showing: epidermis, dermis, ambulacral groove with tube feet (podia), radial water canal running centrally, pyloric caecum, gonads, and coelom. Tube feet labeled with ampullae at top and sucker at bottom

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of phylum Mollusca?
Electric organ is found in which fish?
The diagram shows different types of coelom. Which condition is shown by earthworm?
questions.diagram_description: Four diagrams of animal cross-sections: (A) No body cavity between gut and body wall; (B) body cavity not lined by mesoderm (pseudocoelom); (C) body cavity fully lined by coelomic epithelium derived from mesoderm; (D) haemocoel with open circulatory system

questions.question: Cephalochordata is represented by:
Which one of the following animals is correctly matched with its phylum?
In which of the following, fertilization is external and development is direct?
Book lungs are respiratory organs found in:
Which of the following correctly represents the chordate characters?

options.A: Notochord – pharyngeal gill slits – dorsal hollow nerve cord
options.B: Notochord – ventral solid nerve cord – pharyngeal gill slits
options.C: Dorsal hollow nerve cord – ventral notochord – gill slits
options.D: Post-anal tail – ventral nerve cord – notochord

questions.question: Study the diagram of a generalized Arthropod. Identify the structure that allows moulting.
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of arthropod body showing: chitinous exoskeleton, flexible intersegmental membranes between sclerites, moulting fluid space, epidermis (hypodermis) beneath old cuticle, ecdysone-secreting prothoracic gland location indicated, and new cuticle forming beneath old one

options.A: Flexible intersegmental membrane allows growth, old exoskeleton shed by ecdysis
options.D: Radula helps in breaking the shell

questions.question: Which class of Mammalia is characterized by the presence of a pouch (marsupium)?
Urochordata is also called:
Which of the following is characteristic of class Reptilia but NOT class Amphibia?
The diagram shows the canal system of a sponge. Identify the sycon type.
questions.diagram_description: Three diagrams of sponge canal systems: (A) Ascon type – straight canals from pores to spongocoel; (B) Sycon type – radial canals branching from incurrent canals, choanocytes lining radial canals; (C) Leucon type – complex folded canals with flagellated chambers; arrows show water flow direction in each

questions.question: Parapodia are locomotory organs found in:
Which of the following is a cold-blooded (poikilothermic) animal?
The arrangement of teeth in sockets of jawbones is called:
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of phylum Echinodermata?
Identify the correct sequence of embryonic development in animals: Zygote → ?
The diagram shows the anatomy of Cockroach. How many pairs of legs does it have and to which class does it belong?
questions.diagram_description: Dorsal view diagram of cockroach showing: head with antennae and compound eyes, thorax with pronotum, two pairs of wings (tegmina and hind wings), three pairs of jointed legs (one pair each on prothorax, mesothorax, metathorax), segmented abdomen with cerci

questions.question: Which of the following characters is used to distinguish Aves from Reptilia?
The larval form of Cnidaria that is free-swimming and ciliated is:
Which of the following animals has a four-chambered heart?
